description: From the neck towards the hips, 'rokai' covers the rear region of the torso. 'rokili' (spine) is the central bony column within it; 'noe' (behind) instead locates one thing relative to another.
articulatory_notes: Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge, then draw the tongue back and round the lips for middle-height 'o'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k' and release into stressed open central unrounded 'a'. Raise the tongue into high-front unrounded 'i' for the separate final syllable.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Sitting practice attends to the back as a region that can be upright without being held rigid.

semantic_domains.physical: the rear region of the human torso
